UNKNOWN: Appropriate measurement metrics and timeout values depend on your specific workload and infrastructure details.
Measure incoming request rate (RPS), error rate, and p95 latency per service endpoint.
Track TCP metrics: retransmission count, RTT, and socket buffer utilization.
Monitor server queue lengths and thread pool occupancy to detect backpressure.
Set a connection pool max size based on CPU cores and memory, leaving ~20% headroom.
Configure client connect timeout ~2× typical RTT (e.g., 200 ms) and read timeout ~2× p95 latency.
Implement exponential backoff with jitter for retries; start at 100 ms, double each attempt.
Limit max retries to 3–5 to prevent retry storms while allowing transient recovery.
Use a circuit‑breaker: open if error rate >5% over 30 s, stay open for 60 s before probing.
